leagueBriefing: Kimi Antonelli made history in Shanghai, becoming the second-youngest winner in F1 history while leading a Mercedes 1-2. The race was defined by a teenage prodigy's dominance and a vintage wheel-to-wheel scrap between Ferrari teammates that saw Lewis Hamilton secure his first podium in red.

games.headline: Teenage star Antonelli makes history as Hamilton grabs first Ferrari podium
games.brief: Kimi Antonelli proved the hype is real, dominating the Chinese Grand Prix at just 19 years old to lead home a Mercedes one-two finish. While the Italian youngster checked out at the front, the real fireworks happened behind him as Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc engaged in a thrilling, high-stakes duel for the final podium spot. Hamilton eventually prevailed in the intra-team Ferrari battle to claim his first trophy for the Scuderia, marking a major milestone in his new chapter with the Italian team.
bullets: Kimi Antonelli (19 years, 202 days) became the second-youngest F1 winner ever, trailing only Max Verstappen.
Lewis Hamilton secured his first podium finish since November 2024 and his first ever for Ferrari.
George Russell retained his championship lead by four points over Antonelli after finishing second.
Max Verstappen suffered a rare DNF after an electrical failure forced him out with 10 laps remaining.
It was a disastrous day for McLaren as both Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri failed to start due to power unit issues.
games.context: Antonelli's maiden win signals a massive shift in the championship race as Mercedes continues to dominate the early 2026 season.
